1247	\begin{definition}
1248	
1249	\leanok
1250	[The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\)]
1251	  \label{def:tautological_quotient}
1252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient}
1253	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1254	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle,
1255	    def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
1256	    def:gr_modules_glueHom}
1257	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1258	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1259	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L908-L910).
1260	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1261	  % "This is compatible with the homomorphisms $u^I$,
1262	  %  so we get a surjective homomorphism
1263	  %  $u : \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$."
1264	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1265	  The chart quotients \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1266	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing
1267	  \((g_{I,J})\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}): over
1268	  each overlap \(U^I_J\) one has the identity of \(d \times r\) matrices
1269	  \[
1270	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,
1271	  \]
1272	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1273	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}).
1274	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1275	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1276	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1277	  \[
1278	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1279	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1280	  \]
1281	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1282	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1283	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1284	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1285	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1286	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.
1287	\end{definition}

1289	\section{The functor of points}
1290	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1291	
1292	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1293	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1294	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1295	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1296	identities that make that action functorial.
1297	
1298	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1299	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1300	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1301	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1302	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1303	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1304	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1305	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1306	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1307	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1308	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1309	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1310	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1311	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1312	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1313	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1314	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1315	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1316	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1317	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1318	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1319	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1320	  functor's value at \(T\).
1321	
1322	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1323	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1324	  \[
1325	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1326	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1327	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1328	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1329	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1330	  \]
1331	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1332	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1333	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1334	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1335	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1336	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1337	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1338	  map of equivalence classes.
1339	\end{definition}
1340	
1341	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
1342	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1343	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
1344	
1345	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1346	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1347	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1348	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1349	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1350	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
1351	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
1352	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
1353	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
1354	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1355	\end{lemma}
1356	
1357	\begin{proof}
1358	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1359	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
1360	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
1361	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
1362	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
1363	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
1364	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
1365	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
1366	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
1367	\end{proof}
1368	
1369	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1370	[Identity coherence at the free sheaf]
1371	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}
1372	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id}
1373	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1374	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\) and an index set \(I\), the free-pullback
1375	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1) \cong \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) coincides
1376	  with the component at the free sheaf \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) of the pullback-identity
1377	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1378	\end{lemma}
1379	
1380	\begin{proof}
1381	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1382	  Both sides are cocones on the coproduct \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\); by coproduct
1383	  extensionality it suffices to check equality after precomposing with each coprojection
1384	  \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element case
1385	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}.
1386	\end{proof}
1387	
1388	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1389	[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1390	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1391	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1392	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1393	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
1394	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
1395	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
1396	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
1397	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).
1398	\end{lemma}
1399	
1400	\begin{proof}
1401	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp,
1402	    lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1403	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
1404	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
1405	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
1406	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The compatibility of the mate
1407	  with the adjunction hom-equivalences is supplied by
1408	  \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}, the reusable conjugate-naturality engine. The
1409	  pentagon coherence of the iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation
1410	  well-defined.
1411	\end{proof}
1412	
1413	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1414	[Mate/conjugate compatibility of the adjunction hom-equivalence]
1415	  \label{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1417	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \mathrm{adj}_2\) be two adjunctions and \(\alpha\) a natural
1418	  transformation between their left adjoints. For a morphism \(f\) the hom-equivalence of
1419	  \(\mathrm{adj}_2\) applied to \(\alpha_c \circ f\) equals the hom-equivalence of
1420	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1\) applied to \(f\), post-composed with the component at \(d\) of the
1421	  conjugate (mate) of \(\alpha\). This is the general naturality of the conjugate
1422	  construction against the two hom-equivalences; it is the reusable engine behind
1423	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.
1424	\end{lemma}
1425	
1426	\begin{proof}
1427	  Both sides unfold to the same pasting of unit/counit triangles for the two adjunctions;
1428	  the equality is the standard compatibility of the mate of \(\alpha\) with the
1429	  hom-set bijections.
1430	\end{proof}
1431	
1432	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1433	[Composite coherence at the free sheaf]
1434	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1435	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1436	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1437	  The composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}: for composable \(a, b\) and an
1438	  index set \(I\), the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b \circ a\) at \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\)
1439	  factors through \(a^{*}\) of the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b\), the one for \(a\), and
1440	  the iterated-pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}.
1441	\end{lemma}
1442	
1443	\begin{proof}
1444	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1445	  By coproduct extensionality it suffices to check the identity after precomposing with each
1446	  coprojection \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element
1447	  composite coherence \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp} (with the base-ring sheaf
1448	  tracked through each pullback).
1449	\end{proof}
1450	
1451	Together \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1452	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
1453	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
1454	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
1455	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
1456	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
1457	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
1458	
1459	\begin{definition}
1460	
1461	\leanok
1462	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
1463	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
1464	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
1465	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
1466	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1467	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1468	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1469	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
1470	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1471	  % "represents the
1472	  %  contravariant functor
1473	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1474	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1475	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1476	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1477	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
1478	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1479	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
1480	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
1481	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
1482	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
1483	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
1484	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
1485	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
1486	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1487	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
1488	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
1489	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
1490	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
1491	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
1492	  \[
1493	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
1494	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
1495	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
1496	  \]
1497	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
1498	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
1499	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
1500	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
1501	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
1502	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
1503	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
1504	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
1505	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
1506	\end{definition}
1507	
1508	\section{The universal property}
1509	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
1510	
1511	\begin{theorem}
1512	
1513	\leanok
1514	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
1515	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
1516	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
1517	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
1518	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
1519	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
1520	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1521	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1522	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
1523	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1524	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
1525	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
1526	  %  contravariant functor
1527	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1528	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1529	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1530	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1531	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
1532	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
1533	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
1534	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1535	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
1536	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
1537	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
1538	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
1539	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
1540	  \[
1541	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
1542	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1543	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
1544	    \qquad
1545	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
1546	      \;\longmapsto\;
1547	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
1548	  \]
1549	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme

1551	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf

1553	  universal family.
1554	\end{theorem}

1556	\begin{proof}
1557	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
1558	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
1559	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
1560	    def:grassmannian_functor}
1561	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
1562	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
1563	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
1564	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
1565	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
1566	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
1567	
1568	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
1569	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
1570	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
1571	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
1572	  the composite
1573	  \[
1574	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
1575	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1576	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
1577	  \]
1578	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
1579	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
1580	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
1581	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
1582	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
1583	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
1584	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
1585	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
1586	
1587	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
1588	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
1589	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
1590	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
1591	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
1592	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
1593	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
1594	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
1595	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
1596	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
1597	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
1598	
1599	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
1600	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
1601	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
1602	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
1603	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
1604	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
1605	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
1606	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\), so by the cocycle condition
1607	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
1608	  \(\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). By

1610	  q \rangle\), since this holds on each \(T_I\) and the equivalence class is
1611	  determined by \(\ker(q)\).
1612	
1613	  \emph{Uniqueness.} If \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) also satisfies
1614	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), then
1615	  on \(\psi^{-1}(U^I)\) the pulled-back tautological quotient \(\psi^{*}u^I\)

1617	  hence \(\psi = \varphi_I\) there; as the \(U^I\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\),
1618	  \(\psi = \varphi\). Thus \(\varphi\) is the unique morphism inducing
1619	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), and \(\varphi \mapsto \langle
1620	  \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u \rangle\) is bijective.
